fields.Text:
Gallstone ultrasound with dark posterior artifact. Artifact?

{{c1::Posterior acoustic shadowing}}
fields.Extra:
Q1. Correct; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: Posterior shadowing indicates a strong attenuator, such as a stone or calcification, effectively blocking sound transmission and creating an anechoic (dark) area deeper to it.

fields.Text: Posterior acoustic shadowing indicates a {{c1::strong attenuator such as a stone or calcification}}.

fields.Text: The critical risk factor for nephrogenic systemic fibrosis is {{c1::severe renal dysfunction/AKI after gadolinium exposure}}.
fields.Extra:
Q2. Correct; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: Severely reduced renal function (eGFR <30 mL/min/1.73 m² or acute kidney injury) is the critical risk factor for nephrogenic systemic fibrosis, as NSF occurs almost exclusively in patients with advanced kidney impairment exposed to gadolinium-based contrast agents.




Open review Q2

Left atrial appendage thrombus. Most feared complication?

{{c1::Stroke}}

Q3. Correct; difficulty: not listed. This patient has a large left atrial lumen and left atrial appendage thrombus.

fields.Text: Left atrial appendage thrombus is feared because it can embolize and cause {{c1::stroke}}.

{{c1::Doppler flow in endometrial debris}}

{{c1::The curve closest to the diagonal/no-discrimination line}}

fields.Text:
Cord MRI with dorsal column T2 hyperintensity and paresthesias. Deficiency to evaluate?

{{c1::Vitamin B12/cobalamin deficiency}}
fields.Extra:
Q7. Correct; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: Subacute combined degeneration of the cord (SACD) is caused by vitamin B12 deficiency. Most commonly there is symmetric bilateral high signal within the dorsal columns. This appearance has been described as the inverted "V" sign 4. The signal changes typically begin in the upper thoracic region, with ascending or descending progression.

fields.Text: Subacute combined degeneration is caused by {{c1::vitamin B12/cobalamin deficiency}}.

fields.Text:
Newborn contrast enema with microcolon and distal ileal filling defects. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Meconium ileus}}
fields.Extra:
Q8. Correct; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: Multiple small filling defects in the distal ileum with microcolon is consistent with meconium ileus and should prompt cystic fibrosis testing. The water-soluble contrast can be therapeutic by facilitating passage of inspissated meconium.

fields.Text: Meconium ileus shows {{c1::microcolon with multiple distal ileal filling defects}} and should prompt cystic fibrosis testing.

fields.Text: Horner syndrome in a heavy smoker should prompt concern for a {{c1::right apical/Pancoast lung mass}}.
fields.Extra:
Q10. Correct; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: The triad of ptosis, miosis, and facial anhidrosis classically characterizes Horner syndrome. Horner syndrome is the result of the interruption of the occulosympathetic chain. It may result from apical lung tumors.





Open review Q10

fields.Text: The pediatric diagnostic imaging radiation-reduction initiative is {{c1::Image Gently}}.
fields.Extra:
Q11. Correct; difficulty: not listed. In recognition of the potential risks of radiation from diagnostic imaging procedures, particularly in the pediatric population, the Society for Pediatric Radiology formed a committee in late 2006.




Open review Q11

fields.Text: Gadolinium in pregnancy is {{c1::relatively contraindicated}} and should be used only when benefit clearly outweighs fetal risk.
fields.Extra:
Q12. Correct; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: Gadolinium-based contrast media are FDA pregnancy class C and relatively contraindicated. They cross the placental barrier, are excreted into amniotic fluid where prolonged retention increases risk of gadolinium ion dissociation and should only be administered when benefit clearly outweighs potential risk to the fetus.




Open review Q12

fields.Text: Before direct MR arthrography, obtain a {{c1::complete allergic reaction history}} regardless of injection route.
fields.Extra:
Q13. Correct; difficulty: not listed. A complete allergic history is recommended before performing any radiologic study using contrast material.





Open review Q13

fields.Text:
Post-traumatic ossification adjacent to the medial femoral condyle. Injured structure?

{{c1::Medial collateral ligament}}
fields.Extra:
Q14. Correct; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: Pellegrini-Stieda lesion is post-traumatic ossification adjacent to the medial femoral condyle near the medial collateral ligament insertion. It typically occurs in males following knee trauma. The lesion is usually asymptomatic but becomes Pellegrini-Stieda syndrome when painful.

fields.Text: A Pellegrini-Stieda lesion is post-traumatic ossification near the {{c1::MCL insertion at the medial femoral condyle}}.

{{c1::Subependymal giant cell astrocytoma}}

1/2
fields.Extra:
Q15. Correct; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: Subependymal giant cell astrocytoma appears as a large enhancing mass near the foramen of Monro in patients with tuberous sclerosis. It demonstrates variable T1 and T2 signal intensity with avid enhancement. SEGAs are larger than subependymal nodules and may cause obstructive hydrocephalus.

{{c1::Aortic transection}}

fields.Text: After reduction mammoplasty, residual breast tissue may be redistributed {{c1::inferiorly relative to the elevated nipple}}.
fields.Extra:
Q19. Correct; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: Most of the tissue removed from the breast during mammoplasty is inferior to the nipple, so when post-surgical scarring occurs, it can contribute to retraction of the remaining tissue inferiorly. The nipple is also commonly elevated as part of the mammoplasty procedure, accounting for more superior position of the nipple on subsequent mammography.

fields.Text: Radiation-induced cancer risk from CT is heavily dependent on {{c1::age at exposure}}, with higher risk in children.
fields.Extra:
Q23. Correct; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: The number of computed tomographic (CT) studies performed is increasing rapidly. Because CT scans involve much higher doses of radiation than plain films, we are seeing a marked increase in radiation exposure in the general population. Epidemiologic studies indicate that the radiation dose from even two or three CT scans results in a detectable increase in the risk of cancer, especially in children.

fields.Text:
Newborn with large solid renal mass. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Mesoblastic nephroma}}
fields.Extra:
Q25. Correct; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: Mesoblastic nephroma appears as a large solid intrarenal mass on CT with variable enhancement pattern. Necrosis, hemorrhage, and calcification are uncommon features. It occurs in infants within the first 6 months of life and may show infiltration of perinephric tissues requiring wide surgical margins.

fields.Text:
Chest radiograph with snowman/figure-of-eight configuration. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Supracardiac TAPVC}}
fields.Extra:
Q26. Correct; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: : Supracardiac TAPVC shows the characteristic "snowman" or "figure-of-eight" sign on chest radiograph. The "head" of the snowman is formed by the abnormal vertical vein and dilated superior vena cava, while the "body" is formed by the enlarged right atrium and pulmonary artery. Additional findings include cardiomegaly with prominent pulmonary arteries.

fields.Text:
Postpartum bleeding with persistent beta-hCG and hypervascular endometrial tissue. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Retained products of conception}}

1/2
fields.Extra:
Q28. Incorrect; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: Retained products of conception shows high flow vascularity because residual trophoblastic tissue maintains rich vascular supply from endometrial vessels. Persistently elevated beta-hCG occurs because it continues to be produced by the retained trophoblastic tissue. The vascularity originates from endometrium and extends into myometrium, unlike arteriovenous malformations where flow centers in myometrium.

fields.Text:
Stable mammographic oil cyst/radiolucent-centered masses. Interpretation?

{{c1::Benign fat necrosis}}
fields.Extra:
Q32. Correct; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: Smooth curvilinear calcifications in oil cysts represent benign fat necrosis evolution and are pathognomonic when seen in an appropriate clinical context. Unlike suspicious malignant calcifications (branching, pleomorphic, clustered), these smooth patterns require no further workup and indicate normal post-traumatic or post-surgical healing.

fields.Text:
Adjustable nuclear medicine uptake device shown. Device?

{{c1::Thyroid uptake probe/well counter setup}}
fields.Extra:
Q38. Correct; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: Well counters are fixed instruments where samples are inserted into the crystal well, requiring minimal movement/adjustability. Thyroid probes are highly adjustable and pivotable, mounted on a movable arm to position properly over patients' thyroid glands for uptake measurements. The adjustability of the thyroid probe enables accommodation of varying patient sizes and positions.

{{c1::Rolando fracture}}

fields.Text: A Rolando fracture is a {{c1::comminuted intra-articular fracture at the first metacarpal base}}.

fields.Text:
Filtered x-ray spectrum with discrete spikes. What do the spikes represent?

{{c1::Characteristic radiation}}
fields.Extra:
Q40. Correct; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: Characteristic radiation occurs when incident electrons eject inner shell electrons, creating vacancies filled by outer shell electrons that emit x-ray photons with energies equal to the binding energy differences between the involved electron shells. Discrete monoenergetic peaks are produced, superimposed on the continuous bremsstrahlung spectrum that are specific to the target element and only occur when tube voltage exceeds the inner shell binding energy.

fields.Text: Characteristic radiation produces {{c1::discrete monoenergetic x-ray peaks}} specific to the target element.

Ovarian fat-containing lesion on lumbar MRI. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Ovarian dermoid}}

Abdominal MRI with fatty pancreatic replacement. Diagnosis?

fields.Text:
Young patient with posterior element spine lesion and neck pain. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Osteoblastoma}}

1/2
fields.Extra:
Q46. Correct; difficulty: not listed. The computed tomography (CT) images show a relatively large (greater than 2 cm) predominantly lytic mass, with a sclerotic rim (blue arrow), which is mildly expansile (yellow arrow) and contains osseous matrix (purple arrow).

fields.Text:
Spectral Doppler aliasing artifact. Adjustment to reduce it?

{{c1::Increase PRF}}
fields.Extra:
Q47. Correct; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: Aliasing in spectral Doppler occurs when velocities exceed the Nyquist limit determined by pulse repetition frequency - it can be reduced by increasing PRF, using lower frequency transducers, or switching to continuous wave Doppler for high velocities.




Open review Q47

fields.Text: Spectral Doppler aliasing is reduced by {{c1::increasing PRF}}.
fields.Extra:

Vital Concept: Aliasing in spectral Doppler occurs when velocities exceed the Nyquist limit determined by pulse repetition frequency - it can be reduced by increasing PRF, using lower frequency transducers, or switching to continuous wave Doppler for high velocities.





Open review Q47

fields.Text: Among benign breast lesions, {{c1::fibroadenoma}} is associated with the lowest breast cancer risk increase.
fields.Extra:
Q48. Correct; difficulty: not listed. Fibroadenomas are benign breast lesions.

fields.Text:
Pediatric acute scrotal pain with decreased Doppler flow. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Testicular torsion}}
fields.Extra:
Q49. Correct; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: Pediatric testicular torsion shows decreased or absent blood flow on color Doppler compared to the contralateral testis. Doppler settings must be optimized for low flow detection. Gray-scale appearance may be normal early in torsion, making Doppler assessment critical for diagnosis.





Open review Q49

fields.Text: Pediatric testicular torsion is diagnosed by {{c1::decreased or absent testicular Doppler flow}} compared with the other side.

fields.Text:
Forefoot metatarsal head abnormality. Typical demographic?

{{c1::Adolescent/young female, often high-heel wearer}}
fields.Extra:
Q51. Correct; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: Freiberg's infraction is a repetitive stress fracture of the metatarsal head. It is most commonly seen in females (4:1) and between the ages of 13 and 18 years old. It is most commonly found in the second metatarsal head.





Open review Q51

fields.Text: Freiberg infraction is most common in {{c1::adolescent/young females}} and usually affects the second metatarsal head.

fields.Text:
Mammographic calcifications best detected by 2D/magnification. Lesion?

{{c1::DCIS}}
fields.Extra:
Q52. Correct; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: Mammography with magnification views remains the gold standard for ductal carcinoma in situ detection, as tomosynthesis may miss fine calcifications that characterize this disease.

fields.Text: Tomosynthesis is not better than 2D mammography for detecting {{c1::DCIS/fine calcifications}}.

fields.Text:
Hepatic abscess drainage planning. Safest catheter route?

{{c1::Through normal liver parenchyma}}
fields.Extra:
Q53. Incorrect; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: Percutaneous liver abscess drainage should traverse normal liver parenchyma to make a seal around the drainage catheter, helping to prevent contaminated abscess contents from leaking into the peritoneal cavity, minimizing hemorrhage, and helping to keep the catheter in proper position.




Open review Q53

fields.Text: Percutaneous liver abscess drainage should traverse {{c1::normal liver parenchyma}} to seal the tract and reduce leakage.
fields.Extra:

Open review Q53

Breast calcifications shown. BI-RADS morphology?

{{c1::Coarse heterogeneous}}
fields.Extra:
Q54. Correct; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: Coarse heterogeneous calcifications are of intermediate concern for malignancy while fine pleomorphic calcifications are highly suspicious of malignancy.

fields.Text:
Post-arthroplasty pseudotumor/ALVAL-type complication. Cause?

{{c1::Metallosis}}
fields.Extra:
Q55. Correct; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: Metallosis/ALVAL on MRI post-THA appears as a lobular pseudotumor with T2-hypointensity, intermediate-to-high T1 signal, and a well-defined low-signal rim on both sequences, representing lymphocytic inflammatory reaction to metal debris.





Open review Q55
